samples.xml_description:
Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer. Clock. Reset signal. Defined as 1 for reset and 0 for reset signal inactive. 32-bit input operand A. 32-bit input operand B. 32-bit output representing the accumulated result. Multiply and accumulate in one operation.

Implementation:

The module implements a parallel multiplier and accumulator using registers and an always block.

Multiply and Accumulate (MAC_PE):

The module performs the multiply and accumulate operation. In the MAC_PE, there is a register that stores the partial sum (the intermediate accumulation result), and in each cycle, the result of “a multiplied by b” will be accumulated in this register, and the “c” shows the value of the register.

Accumulator Register:

The module includes a register, c, to store the accumulated result. On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the positive edge of the reset signal (rst), the module updates the register. If the reset signal (rst) is high, indicating a reset condition, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the register (c) is updated by adding the product of the input operands (a and b) to its current value.

Multiplication and Accumulation:

Inside the always block, the module performs the multiplication and accumulation operation. If the reset signal (rst) is high, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the module adds the product of the input operands (a and b) to the register (c).
